question_text: Name two non-contact forces and explain why they are called 'non-contact' forces.
correct_answer: Any two of: gravitational force, magnetic force, electric force. They are called non-contact forces because they can act on objects without the objects physically touching each other.
explanation: Non-contact forces work at a distance through invisible fields around objects. For example, gravity pulls on objects without touching them, and magnets can attract iron from a distance.
